LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAL WELLNESS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE - THE MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS BY "CAL WELLNESS, AN UNINCORPORATED, NON-PROFIT ASSOCIATION" IS DENIED. A REGISTERED PROCESS SERVER DECLARES UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY THAT HE PERSONALLY SERVED CAL WELLNESS VIA "DAVID SANDERSUM" AT "3225 W. SWAIN RD., STOCKTON, CA 95219" ON JULY 24, 2018 AND ALSO MAILED THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT TO THAT ADDRESS. NEVIN EBERHART-SANDSTROM, DECLARES THAT CAL WELLNESS HAS "NEVER USED" THE STOCKTON ADDRESS "AS ITS BUSINESS OR MAILING ADDRESS." HOWEVER, ON FEBRUARY 3, 2017, EBERHARDT-SANDSTROM - ACTING AS "PRESIDENT" OF CAL WELLNESS - FILED A GENERAL DENIAL ON ITS BEHALF THAT USES THE ADDRESS "3225 W. SWAIN RD., STOCKTON, CA, 95219." AT A MINIMUM, SUBSTITUTE SERVICE WAS EFFECTED UNDER CCP 415.20. MOREOVER, IT IS CLEAR THAT CAL WELLNESS HAS NOTICE OF THIS SUIT. COUNSEL FOR WELLNESS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE PROPOSED ORDER IS TO BE IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: MTORREANO@SFTC.ORG. =(302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN MASSEY'S MOTION FOR A JUDGMENT BASED ON THE PLEADINGS WITH LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT IS DENIED. FIRST, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVIDE A PROOF OF SERVICE AND THE ONLY COMPETENT EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT THE MOTION WAS NOT SERVED IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 1005(B). (HONG DEC., PAR. 21.) SECOND, PLAINTIFF'S STATUTORY MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS UNTIMELY BECAUSE IT IS MADE LESS THAN 30-DAYS BEFORE TRIAL. (SEE CCP 438(E).) THIRD, NOTHING IN THE RECORD INDICATES THAT PLAINTIFF SERVED THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ON DEFENDANTS AND DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ANSWERED THAT PLEADING. THE MOTION IS PREMATURE BECAUSE A PLAINTIFF MAY BRING A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS ONLY WHERE "THE COMPLAINT STATES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OR CAUSES OF ACTION AGAINST THE DEFENDANT AND THE ANSWER DOES NOT STATE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A DEFENSE TO THE COMPLAINT." (CCP 438(C)(1)(A).) THE COURT DENIES WITHOUT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFF'S INDIRECT REQUEST TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AS THE MOTION DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1324. THE COURT DENIES WITHOUT PREJUDICE DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR A DISMISSAL BECAUSE SUCH A REQUEST IS NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ANGELA BRADSTREET; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR NO. 12353, EMAIL: MVISCIO@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT CAL WELLNESS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE.

There being no appearance, the Court adopted the tentative ruling:

The motion to quash service of summons by "Cal Wellness, an unincorporated, non-profit association" is DENIED. A registered process server declares under penalty of perjury that he personally served Cal Wellness via "David Sandersum" at "3225 W. Swain Rd., Stockton, CA 95219" on July 24, 2018 and also mailed the summons and complaint to that address. Nevin Eberhart-Sandstrom, declares that Cal Wellness has "never used" the Stockton address "as its business or mailing address." However, on February 3, 2017, Eberhardt-Sandstrom - acting as "president" of Cal Wellness - filed a general denial on its behalf that uses the address "3225 W. Swain Rd., Stockton, CA, 95219." At a minimum, substitute service was effected under CCP 415.20. Moreover, it is clear that Cal Wellness has notice of this suit. Counsel for Wellness is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling. The proposed order is to be in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: mtorreano@sftc.org. =(302/RBU)

Specially Appearing Defendant Cal Wellness'S Motion For Dismissal, Or In The Alternative To Quash.
Off Calendar Jun-29-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Specially Appearing Defendant Cal Wellness'S Motion For Dismissal, Or In The Alternative motion To Quash.

Specially appearing defendant Cal Wellness's motion for dismissal is denied and its alternative motion to quash service of the second amended complaint on counsel for Cal Wellness is granted. The apparent mistake of counsel for plaintiff Stephen Massey in believing that service of the second amended complaint on counsel for Cal Wellness was sufficient to require Cal Wellness to respond to the second amended complaint is not a basis to preclude Mr. Massey from seeking any relief against Cal Wellness, especially given the lack of any legal prejudice to Cal Wellness and California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits. If, as it appears, counsel for Cal Wellness is not authorized or willing to accept service of the summons and second amended complaint on behalf of Cal Wellness, then Mr. Massey will need to effect valid service on Cal Wellness.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-03-2017 Continued To Feb-05-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc And Mtn For A Judgment Based On The Pleadings With Leave To Amend Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Stephen Massey, In Propria Persona, (417-414-9010) appears as self represented plaintiff/moving/opposing party

Attorney, Billy Cosmopulos of the law offices of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (415-288-6300) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF STEPHEN MASSEY's Motion For A Judgment Based On The Pleadings With Leave To Amend Complaint is DENIED. First, plaintiff fails to provide a proof of service and the only competent evidence indicates that the motion was not served in compliance with CCP 1005(b). (Hong Dec., par. 21.) Second, plaintiff's statutory motion for judgment on the pleadings is untimely because it is made less than 30-days before trial. (See CCP 438(e).) Third, nothing in the record indicates that plaintiff served the amended complaint on defendants and defendants have not answered that pleading. The motion is premature because a plaintiff may bring a motion for judgment on the pleadings only where "the complaint states facts sufficient to constitute a cause or causes of action against the defendant and the answer does not state facts sufficient to constitute a defense to the complaint." (CCP 438(c)(1)(A).) The court denies without prejudice plaintiff's indirect request to amend the complaint as the motion does not comply with CRC 3.1324. The court denies without prejudice defendant's request for a dismissal because such a request is not properly before the Court. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Angela Bradstreet; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No. 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)
